alt_forms.note: D&D Japanese translation (Hobby Japan) uses 筋力 for the Strength ability score. More specific to muscular/physical strength.

definitions.gloss_en: a stat representing physical strength; determines carrying capacity, melee damage, or similar physical effects
definitions.gloss_jp: {ユニット|ゆにっと}または{キャラクター|きゃらくたー}の{身|み}{体|たい}{的|てき}な{強|つよ}さを{示|しめ}す{ステータス|すてーたす}
definitions.explanation_en: A fundamental attribute or stat measuring raw physical power. Used in TRPGs and SRPGs for melee effectiveness, load-bearing capacity, and other strength-dependent checks.
definitions.implicit_notes: 力 (chikara) is the most fundamental Japanese word for power/force. In game stats, it usually refers to raw physical strength as opposed to skill (技), speed (速度), or intelligence (知恵).
definitions.orthography_notes: 力 is a single-character fundamental Japanese word meaning power, force, or strength. 筋力 (kinryoku) is the TRPG-specific compound: 筋 (muscle) + 力 (power), used in D&D Japanese translation for the Strength ability score. Both are fully generic.
definitions.game_setting_notes: In TRPGs: 筋力 (Strength ability score, D&D Hobby Japan translation). In wargames: 力 is a simpler, more archaic-flavored stat label. In JRPGs, 力 frequently appears as the ATK or STR stat shorthand.

usage_jp: {力|ちから}ステータス (Strength stat); {筋|きん}{力|りょく}判{定|てい} (Strength check, TRPG)
comparative_notes: 筋力 is the TRPG-standard translation of Strength (D&D). 力 is appropriate for simpler wargame stat profiles or flavour-text usage. Neither is WH-specific.

examples.japanese: {力|ちから}が{高|たか}いユニットは{近|きん}{接|せつ}{攻|こう}{撃|げき}で{有|ゆう}{利|り}だ。
examples.english: High-Strength units have an advantage in melee attacks.

cross_references.note: 近接攻撃 — melee combat where 力 stat applies
攻撃力 — attack power; often derived from 力
